content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach's moves track the live read with notable precision and the sequencing follows the coaching path until the disclosure, at which point the coach correctly pivots to safety without abandoning warmth. Early moves (turn 2-4) calibrate the threshold from analysis to body to felt-loss, earning each step rather than racing ahead. When the client says 'loss' (turn 5), the coach doesn't milk it but holds. Crucially, when the suicidal disclosure arrives, the coach refuses the client's retraction ('please don't walk that back') — exactly the right move at exactly the right moment — and then sequences: acknowledge → assess frequency/quality → name the pattern of minimizing → ask about existing support → concrete bridging action (write it down, move up the appointment) → resource (988) only when the client asks for it. The 988 offer is earned rather than reflexive, which is the difference between safety-as-care and safety-as-control. The coach also holds silence well, lets the client's own insights land, and consistently mirrors what the client just produced before moving.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Calibrates threshold: distinguishes peace from flatness, then drops into a body question — appropriate opening move for the read."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Notices the blank + jump-to-analysis and names it as possibly part of the pattern; then offers the precise binary 'rest or loss?' which lands. Move matched to read."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Critical pivot moment handled correctly: refuses the retraction, asks directly without alarm, names that he won't let the moment be managed away. This is the right move at the right moment."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names that this is bigger than the session can hold, asks about existing therapist, and asks what would get in the way — surfacing the minimization pattern as obstacle to safety."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Concrete experiment: write down the actual words to bring to therapist. Frames as experiment to defeat the predicted minimizing — sophisticated move."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches 'a few weeks feels like a long time' and uses it; offers a specific scripted sentence to lower threshold of asking for sooner appointment."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Resource offered when client asks, plus reflection that the asking itself is the part-that-wants-to-live. Closes with a check-in question."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["calibrate-the-threshold", "Are you safe right now?", "Is there someone in your life you can call?"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "pleasure-capacity", "stages-of-emotional-development"],
  "notes": "Territory misses are appropriate — once the disclosure arrived, methodology work was correctly subordinated to safety. The coach avoided both anti-patterns: did not catastrophize and did not weaponize safety to control."
}
